vitals.terrain: Alpine road cycling with multiple high-altitude passes

course_description.overview: The Galibier Challenge is a premier high-mountain cyclosportive featuring the iconic Col du Galibier (2,642m), one of the Tour de France's most legendary climbs. The 115 km main event encompasses five major Alpine ascents with 3,350m of elevation gain, tackling some of the most storied terrain in professional cycling.

key_segments.name: Col du Galibier
key_segments.description: The signature 2,642m pass featuring postcard-perfect Alpine landscapes with walls of snow visible just days after the official summer opening

key_segments.name: Col du Télégraphe
key_segments.description: Historic Tour de France climb preceding the Galibier ascent

key_segments.name: Five total Alpine ascents
key_segments.description: Multiple high-altitude passes combining to create 3,350m total elevation gain

course_description.difficulty_narrative: This is a challenging high-mountain cyclosportive designed for experienced cyclists. The combination of five significant Alpine climbs, sustained elevation gain, and high-altitude terrain (culminating at 2,642m) demands strong climbing fitness and mental resilience. The event is timed and competitive, with electronic timing, category-based prize categories, and instant SMS classification updates.

logistics.getting_there: Located in Saint-Michel-de-Maurienne in the Savoie region of the French Alps. Accessible via major Alpine routes; nearest significant city is Chambéry.
logistics.accommodation: Saint-Michel-de-Maurienne and surrounding Maurienne Valley offer lodging options. The event spans June 6-7, allowing for weekend travel.
logistics.registration_details: Online registration only; no on-site registration. Main Cyclosportive (115 km) pricing: €50 (Oct 1-Dec 31), €55 (Jan 1-Apr 30), €60 (May 1-31). Registration closes May 31 at 23:59 or at 800 bib limit. Includes welcome gift, handlebar plate, electronic timing, aid stations, security, hot meal, finisher medal, and baggage service. Optional: Pasta Party (€), Kalas cycling jersey, accident insurance, cancellation insurance.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike recommended for high-altitude Alpine climbing. Baggage service available. Event is a mass participation event registered with Fédération Française de Cyclisme; valid cycling license or recognized certificate required.
logistics.local_considerations: Event occurs early in Alpine season (June 6-7), shortly after Col du Galibier officially opens for summer. Weather can be variable at high altitude. The summit road is closed to motorized vehicles during the event. A new addition for 2026 is 'La Mauriennaise' randonnée (82 km or 65 km options) on Saturday, June 6, offering a non-competitive discovery ride through Maurienne and Valmeinier valleys.

final_verdict.one_liner: A prestigious Alpine cyclosportive that delivers authentic Tour de France climbing on two of cycling's most iconic passes, ideal for experienced riders seeking high-altitude challenge with strong community atmosphere.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced road cyclists with solid climbing fitness seeking to tackle legendary Tour de France terrain. Best suited for riders comfortable with sustained Alpine climbing, high altitude (2,642m), and competitive timed events. The new Saturday randonnée option accommodates less competitive participants.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for serious Alpine cyclists. The prestige of climbing Col du Galibier and Col du Télégraphe in organized mass participation, combined with excellent event organization (98% satisfaction), strong community, and authentic Tour de France terrain justifies the entry cost. The early-season timing (just after official opening) adds unique appeal. Less valuable for casual riders due to difficulty and cost.
pro_tips: Register early (Oct-Dec pricing is €50 vs €60 in May) and aim for early registration window to secure your bib before 800-bib limit
Arrive Saturday for the new Mauriennaise randonnée to acclimatize and enjoy the Pasta Party social event
Prepare for variable high-altitude weather; bring layers and be ready for potential snow/cold conditions at 2,642m elevation
Consider the optional Kalas 'Galibier 2642' cycling jersey as a meaningful souvenir of this iconic climb
